title: numpy 2.4.6 broke sklearn import
action: Downgraded numpy to 2.2.6, rebuilt sklearn, numexpr, bottleneck against new numpy.

title: qwen3vl32b sampling workers crashed on numpy import
action: All sampling workers from June 2 died with numpy ImportError before env was fixed. Chain proceeded with empty data through filter/format/training (all failed), then stuck in 'until adapter exists' loop. Killed stuck chain, cleaned stale empty files, restarted master queue with corrected Phase A.

title: maximal_prompting_ablation.sh used --input instead of --preds
action: preds_to_traces.py argparse expects --preds, so the conversion step silently failed → judge had no input → no metrics.json. Inference itself (1056 items) was fine. Patched the script + same fix applied to prompt_sensitivity_ablation.sh (pre-emptive, hadn't run yet). Re-ran conversion + judge for the existing 1056 maxprompt predictions.
affected: prompt_sensitivity_ablation (will use fixed script when it runs)

title: Dashboard false-positive: paraphrase-ablation training mis-labeled as 'qwen35 v4 LoRA training'
action: Dashboard's running-job detection matched the substring 'sft_lora_qwen35' in any config path. Paraphrase ablation uses sft_lora_qwen35_v4_noparap.yaml, which matched. Tightened the check to require exactly 'sft_lora_{tag}_v4.yaml' AND v4 adapter not yet present.
affected: Live state section — false v4 training label

title: Silent failure: phase A → missing qwen3vl32b v4 metrics
action: Auto-detected by dashboard. Known cause: qwen3vl32b chain was killed mid-run on June 3 ~07:00 due to a numpy/sklearn import crash (workers spawned before env fix). Phase A's 'done' marker fired because the killed bash exited. The chain has been re-inserted as step 0a of Phase C (extended_chains.sh) so it WILL run after the current ablations. Status will flip to ✅ when Phase C populates the expected outputs. | RESOLVED 2026-06-04T01:34:24Z: detector schema fixed — qwen3vl32b outputs are now expected from Phase C (extended_chains), not from the dead Phase A. The chain itself is currently running inside Phase C; when it produces outputs the silent-failure detector won't fire for them. This entry was a stale architecture artifact.
affected: qwen3vl32b v4 chain — re-queued in Phase C step 0a (extended_chains.sh).
Master queue auto-advanced past Phase A; not a re-run of Phase A itself.

title: prompt_sensitivity_ablation runs 6 configs sequentially → blocks master queue ~18h
action: The 6 (model × prompt) combinations each take ~3h on 8 GPUs, totaling ~18h. Master queue was blocked at 'phase B prompt_sensitivity_ablation start' from Jun 3 21:34 UTC and only completed 1/6 configs by Jun 4 01:30 UTC (4h in, ~14h remaining). Each config uses all 8 GPUs, so true parallelism is not possible. Going to (a) let it continue (paper-critical numbers) but (b) accept the long wall-clock since the alternative is dropping prompt-sensitivity entirely. Dashboard now flags any phase running >2× its expected budget so this kind of slow drift is auto-surfaced. | RESOLVED 2026-06-04T01:30:10Z: Killed the script at the 4h mark (1 of 6 configs completed: qwen35_prompt_neutral at full 1056 items). Master queue auto-advanced to Phase C extended_chains, which started qwen3vl32b chain. Trade: lost 5 of 6 prompt-sensitivity cells for open-weight Qwen, gained 15h earlier Phase C start. Closed-API prompt-sensitivity ablation (3 APIs × 3 prompts, GPU-free) still running and provides the cross-family prompt-sensitivity coverage needed for §6.2.
affected: Master queue Phase B (still progressing, slow)

title: Dashboard silent-failure detector did not catch 'phase running unusually long'
action: Original silent_failures() only checked 'phase done + output missing'. It missed 'phase started but running for many hours past expected budget' — exactly the prompt_sensitivity case. Added a PHASE_BUDGET_H map and a stalled-phase check; now any phase running >2× its budgeted wall-clock is auto-flagged in the dashboard's Silent Failures section.

title: Killed open-weight prompt_sensitivity ablation to unblock Phase C (qwen3vl32b chain)
action: Phase B prompt_sensitivity had completed only 1 of 6 configs after 4h; remaining 5 configs needed ~15h more, blocking the qwen3vl32b v4 chain (Phase C step 0a) and downstream small-model + FFT runs. Killed the wrapper bash; killed 8 orphan run_qwen_video workers that were holding GPU memory. Master queue auto-advanced to Phase C. Net trade: 5 lost open-weight prompt-sensitivity cells (qwen35_fewshot/explicit, qwen36_neutral/fewshot/explicit) vs 15h faster Phase C completion. Closed-API prompt-sensitivity (6 cells across GPT-4o/Gemini-3-Flash/Gemini-3.5-Flash, running in parallel, $5 cost) provides the cross-family prompt-sensitivity coverage for §6.2.
affected: Phase C unblocked — qwen3vl32b chain started
